text.title: Information Architecture (Designer Surface) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Information Architecture (Designer Surface). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Designer-surface IA work consumes the ia-framework decisions as input; it does not re-derive them.
rule.rationale: Re-deriving framework decisions wastes effort and produces drift.

rule.statement: Navigation spec names the exact design-system component for each navigation type and ties variants to breakpoints.
rule.rationale: Specs without component bindings drift visually and behaviourally.

rule.statement: Spacing, depth, and hierarchy use design-system tokens, not pixel literals.
rule.rationale: Pixel literals fragment the hierarchy guarantees.

rule.statement: Each IA cluster (category, audience, topic) gets a page-template that designers and engineers reuse.
Engineering handover includes sitemap, nav spec, page-template inventory, and a checklist linking each item to its component.
rule.rationale: Incomplete handovers create silent IA divergence.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-information-architecture.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
